question: Did the model select Option C as the final correct answer?
Did the model correctly conclude that the centripetal accelerations of balls A and B are equal ($a_A = a_B$)?
Did the model correctly determine that the linear velocity of ball A is greater than ball B ($v_A > v_B$)?
Did the model correctly determine that the angular velocity of ball A is less than ball B ($\omega_A < \omega_B$)?
Does the reasoning correctly identify that only gravity ($mg$) and the normal force ($F_N$) act on the balls (explicitly excluding friction due to the smooth surface)?

Does the reasoning correctly analyze the force components, stating or implying that the vertical component of the normal force balances gravity and the horizontal component provides the centripetal force?

Does the reasoning derive or state that centripetal acceleration depends only on gravitational acceleration and the cone angle (e.g., $a = g \tan\theta$), implying it is independent of mass and radius?
Does the reasoning correctly identify from the geometry that the radius of ball A's path is greater than that of ball B ($R_A > R_B$)?
Does the reasoning correctly use the relationship between velocity, acceleration, and radius (e.g., $v = \sqrt{aR}$) to conclude $v_A > v_B$?
Does the reasoning correctly use the relationship between angular velocity, acceleration, and radius (e.g., $\omega = \sqrt{a/R}$) to conclude $\omega_A < \omega_B$?
If a free-body diagram is generated, is the gravity vector ($mg$) drawn pointing vertically downwards?
If a free-body diagram is generated, is the normal force ($F_N$) drawn perpendicular to the conical surface (pointing obliquely upward and away from the surface)?
If a vector diagram is generated, is the resultant (centripetal) force shown pointing horizontally towards the central axis of the cone?
Does the generated image correctly depict ball A at a higher vertical position (and thus a larger radius) than ball B?
If multiple images are generated, are the orientation of the conical surface and the relative positions of the balls consistent across steps?
Do the labels for forces used in the text explanation (e.g., $mg, F_N, F_{net}$) match the labels present in the generated images?
Does the generated image align with the text by correctly omitting friction vectors (consistent with the "smooth surface" description)?
If the text describes a vector triangle relationship (e.g., $mg, F_N, F_{net}$), does the generated image accurately depict this geometry?
